title: Component Editor
text: Component Editor Use the Component Editor to construct application components, such as dynamic and static server pages, and services. When you create or open a dynamic server page, a static server page, or a service, it is opened in its own Component Editor, as a tab in the central editing area. Worksheets Define Structure —define the data that the component uses. Insert Entities and Fields from the Toolbox, and arrange them in a structure that reflects a specific view of the data (up or down). The view can affect the component's behavior in retrieving, storing, and deleting data using the component. For example, a component for a shopping cart or order form may use Modeled Entities for CART and ITEM data, and a non-database entity that defines buttons such as Delete and Go to Checkout. Design Layout —for dynamic and static server pages, create the layout using HTML and CSS. Write Script —define the component behavior using ProcScript and JavaScript (in some circumstances). Each worksheet contains its own Resource Browser and editing tools, appropriate to the activity. How to Construct a Component Using the Uniface IDE Working with Development Objects Figure 1. Component Editor for Dynamic Server Page: Write Script Worksheet Related Links Component Data Structure Every component has a data structure that defines its component entities and fields, and the path required to access the data. The data structure affects the validation rules for data I/O, such as enforcing referential integrity constraints. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
